wins.event: FY 2025 net income $21.3B; diluted EPS grew 17% YoY; FY ROTCE 15% (up from 8% in Q4 2020)
Assets +11% YoY; broad-based loan growth; deposits grew across both consumer and commercial
Returned $23B to shareholders in 2025 (dividend +13%, $18B buybacks); net charge-offs declined 16% YoY
Opened ~3M new credit card accounts (+21% YoY); auto loans +19% YoY; 700 branches refurbished
M&A ranking improved to 8th from 12th; IB deal pipeline highest in 5 years; Premier balances +14%

risks.event: Buyback guidance lowered — Q4 2026 share repurchases expected to be 'lower' than 2025's $18B run-rate
Path/timing to 17-18% ROTCE depends on rates, macro, and regulatory environment
Recently added trading assets are lower margin (though lower risk and less capital intensive)
Continuing reduction of Home Lending portfolio (servicing -$90B in 2025 alone)

guidance.notes: Reaffirmed 17-18% ROTCE medium-term target (set last quarter); buyback pace expected to be lower in 2026 to fund growth; CET1 target 10-10.5%.

_notes: Strong execution across the board with explicit forward-buyback reduction (consequence of growth pivot, not weakness). CEO confident, citing 'position of strength'. Realised excess_5d -0.5% — small noise miss; the buyback-reduction guidance is the only explicit caution that pipelines may have under-weighted.
